name: Arstern Analytics
description: Arstern Analytics is a Nairobi-based data consultancy delivering MEAL system design, market intelligence, and data science services to NGOs, development organizations, and businesses across Kenya and East Africa.
about: Arstern Analytics is built on the founder's cross-disciplinary expertise in criminology, information management, cybersecurity, and development sector MEAL — combining rigorous data methodology with on-the-ground programme experience. The firm targets a growing gap in quality, affordable data services across East Africa's NGO and SME ecosystem.

revenue_streams.name: MEAL consultancy
revenue_streams.description: M&E system design, data collection, reporting for NGOs

revenue_streams.name: Market intelligence
revenue_streams.description: Nairobi demand intelligence, sector trend reports

revenue_streams.name: Training & capacity building
revenue_streams.description: Data analysis, KoboToolbox, Python for MEAL

plan.executive_summary: Arstern Analytics is a Nairobi-based data consultancy delivering MEAL system design, market intelligence, and data science training to development sector organizations and businesses. The firm targets a growing gap in quality, affordable data services across East Africa's NGO and SME ecosystem.
plan.problem_solution: NGOs and SMEs in Kenya struggle with weak M&E systems, poor data quality, and limited analytical capacity. Arstern Analytics bridges this with end-to-end MEAL services — from tool design (KoboToolbox, ODK) to dashboard visualization (Looker Studio, Python) — at consultancy rates accessible to mid-tier organizations.
plan.target_market: Primary: Local and international NGOs operating in Kenya and East Africa requiring MEAL system support. Secondary: SMEs and startups needing market intelligence and data-driven decision support. Tertiary: Development sector professionals seeking upskilling in data tools.
plan.revenue_model: Three streams: (1) Project-based MEAL consultancy fees billed per engagement. (2) Retainer contracts with NGOs for ongoing data management. (3) Training workshops billed per cohort. Target: KES 150,000/month by end of Year 1 through a mix of 2–3 active consultancy projects.
plan.competitive_advantage: Rare combination of MEAL expertise, data science capability, IT governance, and cybersecurity knowledge. Built-in credibility through prior NGO programme work (BlueBiz, KWETU). Ability to deliver end-to-end — from data collection to dashboard to narrative report — without needing multiple vendors.
plan.risks_mitigation: Key risks: slow client acquisition as a new firm, income inconsistency from project-based billing, and competition from established consultancies. Mitigation: leverage existing NGO network for early contracts, diversify across retainers and training, and build a public portfolio to demonstrate capability.

name: TOCARE
description: TOCARE is an NGO concept focused on community care, social welfare, and development programming for vulnerable populations in Kenya.
about: TOCARE is being developed as a non-governmental organization with a mission centred on care, dignity, and community empowerment. The concept draws on the founder's development sector experience across programme design, MEAL, and data management to build a credible and impact-driven organization.

revenue_streams.name: Donor grants
revenue_streams.description: Institutional funding from bilateral and multilateral donors

revenue_streams.name: Community programmes
revenue_streams.description: Funded social welfare and development projects

revenue_streams.name: Partnerships
revenue_streams.description: Co-implementation with NGO and government partners

plan.executive_summary: TOCARE is a Kenyan NGO concept focused on community care and social welfare programming. It aims to deliver evidence-based interventions for vulnerable populations through well-structured programmes anchored in strong MEAL systems.
plan.problem_solution: Many Kenyan communities lack access to quality social welfare support. TOCARE addresses this through targeted community programmes, leveraging strong M&E systems to demonstrate impact to donors and stakeholders.
plan.target_market: Vulnerable populations in Kenya — youth, women, and low-income households. Institutional donors and foundations seeking credible local implementing partners with strong accountability frameworks.
plan.revenue_model: Grant-based funding from bilateral donors (USAID, EU, FCDO), multilateral agencies (UNICEF, UNDP), and private foundations. Co-funding arrangements with government line ministries for community programmes.
plan.competitive_advantage: Strong institutional documentation, donor-ready MEAL systems, and deep development sector experience from the outset. Positions TOCARE as a credible, accountable partner from day one rather than building these systems retrospectively.
plan.risks_mitigation: Key risks: competitive grant environment, long lead time to first funding, and capacity constraints as a startup NGO. Mitigation: begin with small sub-grants, partner with established NGOs, and build a demonstrable track record through community-level programming.
